title: salvia pratensis subsp. laciniosa
intro: Salvia pratensis subsp. laciniosa, also known as large-leaved meadow sage, is a subspecies of perennial herb in the mint family native to Europe and Western Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its potential to soothe digestive issues and reduce stress. The health benefits of Salvia pratensis subsp. laciniosa include its ability to improve respiratory function, boost the immune system, and promote a good night's sleep. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol to extract its active compounds.
